explanation: This command uses the required -u and -p flags with ^USER^ and ^PASS^ as specified in the Sigma rule. It is a typical Hydra command for HTTP brute-forcing.
This command uses quotes and escape characters to bypass simple string-based detection while maintaining the same functionality as the original command.
This command adds the -f flag (stop after first valid login) which is optional but does not affect the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
This command explicitly specifies the default port (-s 80) to test whether the rule is sensitive to such optional parameters.
This command adds the --disable-logout flag, which is optional and does not interfere with the Sigma rule's detection logic.
